tagline: Wall Street intelligence game — real data, paper scoring, honest learning.
disclaimer: Paper trading simulation only. Not investment advice. No real-money order placement. Past paper scores do not predict future results.

glossary.definition: A share of ownership in a company. When you own a stock, you own a small piece of that business and may benefit if the company grows.
A short symbol (like AAPL or NVDA) used to identify a stock on exchanges. Tickers make it easy to look up prices and news.
Market capitalization = share price × total shares. It measures how big a company is in dollar terms.
How many shares traded in a period. High volume means many buyers and sellers were active.
Today's volume compared to recent average. Unusual activity (RVOL > 1) can signal attention or news.
A group of companies in the same industry (e.g. Technology, Energy, Financials). Events often affect whole sectors.
An event that could move a stock — earnings, FDA approval, merger, rate decision, or major news.
Official documents companies submit to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. Public and free via EDGAR.
A current report filed when something important happens between quarterly reports — acquisitions, CEO changes, material events.
Percent change from prior close to current price. Large gaps often follow overnight news.
How many Canadian dollars one U.S. dollar buys. Matters for trade, oil, and Canadian companies.
Oil prices affect energy stocks, airlines, inflation, and currencies like CAD. Energy shocks ripple across sectors.
Higher rates tend to hurt growth/tech and real estate; help banks; affect bond prices and borrowing costs.
Two things moving together does not prove one caused the other. Always ask for the mechanism.
A compelling narrative can still be a poor trade if the news is priced in or the risk/reward is poor.
When a model memorizes past data instead of learning patterns that generalize. It can look great in backtests and fail live.
Picking stocks at random. If AI cannot beat random over time, the system must show that honestly.
Winning one week does not prove a strategy works. Sample size and regime matter.
Simulated profit/loss using real delayed quotes — no real orders or money at risk.
NLP compound score on news headlines. Positive/negative tone — not a guarantee of price direction.

symbol_guides.headline: Human baseline tracks portfolio or SPY: NVDA
Graph chain: Mark Carney → Canada → BAM

education.models_overview: Ten benchmark players compete on the same watchlist: Random, SPY, QQQ, Equal-Weight, Momentum, News-Only, Macro, Filing, Entity Graph, and Ensemble. Humans paper-trade alongside them. Paper scoring only — not investment advice.

cards.title: SEC 8-K — Material Events
cards.what_happened: A company filed an 8-K current report with the SEC.
cards.why_it_matters: 8-Ks disclose events that shareholders should know about between quarterly reports.
cards.what_to_watch: Read the form type, check if it mentions earnings, leadership, acquisitions, or financing.

cards.title: Interest Rates and Markets
cards.what_happened: Macro data shows Fed Funds rate, yields, or inflation readings.
cards.why_it_matters: Rates affect borrowing costs, stock valuations, and sector rotation.
cards.what_to_watch: Tech vs banks vs utilities — different sectors react differently to rate changes.

cards.title: Oil Price Connections
cards.what_happened: Oil prices moved or energy-related news appeared.
cards.why_it_matters: Oil affects energy producers, airlines, inflation, and currencies like CAD.
cards.what_to_watch: XLE, airlines, Canadian exporters, and inflation-sensitive sectors.

cards.title: USD/CAD and Global Trade
cards.what_happened: The U.S. dollar moved against the Canadian dollar.
cards.why_it_matters: Canada is a major commodity exporter — CAD often tracks oil and trade flows.
cards.what_to_watch: Canadian banks, energy names, and multinationals with cross-border revenue.

cards.title: Reading a Headline
cards.what_happened: A fresh headline hit the news radar.
cards.why_it_matters: News can move prices — but markets may have already priced it in.
cards.what_to_watch: Which tickers and sectors are tagged? Is the source credible? How fresh is it?

cards.title: AI Paper Predictions
cards.what_happened: An AI agent made a paper hypothesis — not investment advice.
cards.why_it_matters: Agents compete on the same data. Compare them to random and SPY baselines.
cards.what_to_watch: Confidence, reasons, risks, and whether the Skeptic flagged stale data.

active_challenge.title: Filing Detective Challenge
active_challenge.prompt: Does this SEC filing look important or routine? 8-K for ???
active_challenge.hint: 8-K material events vs routine N-CEN/NPORT filings.

careers.title: Future Builder
careers.subtitle: Careers this game teaches — no real money required.

careers.title: AI Engineer
careers.description: Builds AI systems that process data, make predictions, and power intelligent applications.
careers.marketquest_teaches: Agent Debate, Ensemble Agent, AI Tool Scout, Reality Engine
careers.beginner_project: Build a simple news sentiment scorer for one ticker

careers.title: Data Scientist
careers.description: Analyzes data to find patterns, build models, and communicate insights.
careers.marketquest_teaches: Reality Scores, Learning Lab, benchmark comparisons, event radar
careers.beginner_project: Compare agent hit rates vs random baseline over 2 weeks of snapshots

careers.title: Quant Researcher
careers.description: Develops systematic trading strategies using math, data, and backtesting.
careers.marketquest_teaches: Benchmark players, momentum baseline, multi-horizon outcomes
careers.beginner_project: Implement a transparent momentum rule and compare to SPY

careers.title: Business Analyst
careers.description: Connects business problems to data and helps teams make better decisions.
careers.marketquest_teaches: Event Radar, entity graph, lesson cards, filing events
careers.beginner_project: Write a one-page brief on how an 8-K filing affects a sector

careers.title: Portfolio Analyst
careers.description: Tracks holdings, performance, and risk for investment portfolios.
careers.beginner_project: Track a paper portfolio vs SPY for one month

careers.title: Economist
careers.description: Studies how economies work — growth, inflation, employment, and policy.
careers.marketquest_teaches: Macro Agent, FRED data, cross-asset strip, currency connections
careers.beginner_project: Explain how a Fed rate change might affect three different sectors

careers.title: Risk Manager
careers.description: Identifies and measures risks in portfolios and trading operations.
careers.marketquest_teaches: Skeptic Agent, stale-data flags, drawdown scoring, honesty disclosures
careers.beginner_project: List 5 reasons a hyped headline trade could fail

careers.title: Financial Software Developer
careers.description: Builds trading platforms, data pipelines, and financial applications.
careers.marketquest_teaches: Reality Engine, data providers, API routes, freshness layer
careers.beginner_project: Build a watchlist dashboard that shows data freshness badges

careers.title: Product Manager
careers.description: Defines what to build, prioritizes features, and connects users to value.
careers.marketquest_teaches: Full game loop — events to picks to scoring to learning
careers.beginner_project: Design a new mini-challenge and write acceptance criteria

careers.title: Entrepreneur
careers.description: Starts businesses by identifying problems and building solutions people need.
careers.marketquest_teaches: AI Tool Scout, research registries, building with free tools
careers.beginner_project: Research 3 free market data APIs and pitch a student-friendly app idea

careers.title: Public Policy Analyst
careers.description: Studies how government decisions affect economies, industries, and people.
careers.marketquest_teaches: Government feeds, macro events, public figure agent, entity graph
careers.beginner_project: Trace how a policy headline connects to sector tickers via entity graph

careers.title: Market Research Analyst
careers.description: Studies markets, competitors, and consumer trends to guide business strategy.
careers.marketquest_teaches: News radar, sentiment, sector mapping, lesson cards
careers.beginner_project: Map 5 headlines to affected sectors with reasoning

careers.footer_note: You can learn valuable skills and build useful tools without trading real money.

entries.name: Cursor
entries.description: AI-powered IDE for building apps with agent assistance.

entries.name: Local LLM (Ollama)
entries.description: Run open models locally for privacy and experimentation.
entries.notes: Good for student labs without cloud API costs.

entries.name: MCP Servers
entries.description: Standard for connecting AI agents to data sources and tools.
entries.notes: Used in Cursor for browser and backend control.

entries.name: FinRL
entries.description: Open-source framework for financial reinforcement learning — educational, not financial advice.
entries.notes: Explicitly not a recommendation to trade real money.

entries.name: FinGPT
entries.notes: Requires GPU for full models; smaller variants for demos.

entries.name: VADER Sentiment
entries.description: Rule-based sentiment analysis for social/text — used in News Agent.
entries.notes: Already used via news/sentiment_scorer in repo.

entries.name: FinBERT
entries.description: BERT fine-tuned on financial text for sentiment.
entries.notes: Upgrade path from VADER for News-Only Agent.

entries.name: Llama 3 (local)
entries.description: General-purpose local LLM for explanation and reasoning tasks.
entries.notes: Run via Ollama for student-safe local inference.

entries.name: Finnhub
entries.description: Free tier: stock quotes, forex, crypto, news, fundamentals.

entries.name: FRED
entries.description: Official U.S. economic data — rates, inflation, oil, USD/CAD.
entries.notes: FRED_API_KEY optional; some series need key.

entries.name: SEC EDGAR
entries.description: Official SEC APIs for company filings and XBRL data.

entries.name: Alpha Vantage
entries.description: Stock, forex, crypto APIs with free tier.

entries.name: OpenBB
entries.description: Open financial data platform — used in Pencil/acquisition track.
entries.notes: Separate from MarketQuest providers; potential future unify.

entries.name: yfinance
entries.description: Unofficial Yahoo Finance data — fallback quotes (DELAYED).
entries.notes: Used as DELAYED fallback in MarketQuest.

research_report.report:
# MarketQuest Research Scout — 2026-05-20

Total registry entries: 17
- Integrated: 5
- Evaluated: 4
- Not started: 8

## Integrated
- **VADER Sentiment** (sentiment_models) — Rule-based sentiment analysis for social/text — used in News Agent.
- **Finnhub** (market_data_apis) — Free tier: stock quotes, forex, crypto, news, fundamentals.
- **FRED** (macro_data_tools) — Official U.S. economic data — rates, inflation, oil, USD/CAD.
- **SEC EDGAR** (sec_filing_tools) — Official SEC APIs for company filings and XBRL data.
- **yfinance** (market_data_apis) — Unofficial Yahoo Finance data — fallback quotes (DELAYED).

## Ideas to explore next
- Local LLM (Ollama): Offline sentiment scoring, Local agent experiments
- Qlib: Feature store patterns, Model training pipelines
- FinGPT: Finance-tuned sentiment, News summarization
- backtesting.py: Simple strategy backtests, Transparent rule evaluation
- Backtrader: Event-driven backtesting, Multi-data feeds
